report:
=== VISUAL_FINDINGS ===
Mediastinal surgical clips are seen. The cardiac silhouette is top-normal to mildly enlarged. The aorta is calcified and tortuous. Partially imaged is spinal fusion hardware within the thoracolumbar spine. No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen.

=== CLINICAL_EVIDENCE ===
Retrieved literature: FINDINGS: Patient is status post median sternotomy and CABG. Cardiac silhouette size remains top normal. Mediastinal and hilar contours are similar. Pulmonary vasculature is normal. No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax is present. Partially imaged is spinal fusion hardware within the thoracolumbar spine. IMPRESSION: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality.

=== DEDUCTIVE_reasonING ===
Step 1: The presence of mediastinal surgical clips suggests previous thoracic surgery.
Step 2: The mildly enlarged cardiac silhouette may be consistent with the expected changes after CABG.
Step 3: The calcification and tortuosity of the aorta can be considered age-related or related to previous atherosclerotic disease.
Step 4: The partially imaged spinal fusion hardware is consistent
